content.problemStatement:
You are given a C++ program that performs division operations on user-provided integers. Here is the Program Description:

- The provided C++ program initializes two integers, numerator = 1 and denominator = 0, and attempts to divide numerator by denominator. Exception handling is implemented to catch potential errors during this division process.
- The program first checks if the denominator is equal to zero. If it is, a std::runtime_error exception is thrown with the message "Division by zero is not allowed."
- If no exceptions are thrown, the program calculates the result of the division and displays it.
- The program includes three catch blocks:
  - The first catch block (catch (const std::runtime_error& ex)) handles exceptions of type std::runtime_error.
  - The second catch block (catch (const std::invalid_argument& ex)) handles exceptions of type std::invalid_argument.
  - The third catch block (catch (...)) serves as a catch-all for any other unexpected exceptions.\

### Task

Run the code and predict which of the catch block executes.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
